title: [P1][High Impact] Runtime & Intrinsics: stdlib intrinsic migration backlog
description:
Auto-seeded from Molt codebase TODO contracts.

Grouped from 337 leaf items into one active category issue for Runtime & Intrinsics.

Source of truth: codebase TODO contracts under src/, runtime/, tools/, tests/, formal/, and demo/.
Impact: High Impact (score 10643).
Pressure: P0: 0, P1: 322, P2: 8, P3: 7.
Status pressure: missing: 0, partial: 11, planned: 326.
Codebase-backed pressure: 337 leaf items.
Secondary non-code pressure: 0 leaf items.
Owners: runtime, stdlib.
Milestones: SL1, SL2, SL3, TL3.
Representative sources: src/molt/stdlib/_aix_support.py:13; src/molt/stdlib/_android_support.py:13; src/molt/stdlib/_apple_support.py:13; src/molt/stdlib/_ast.py:13; src/molt/stdlib/_ast_unparse.py:13.

Leaf inventory:
- [P1][SL2] complete native re parity and continue migrating parser/matcher execution into Rust (named-group edge cases, verbose-mode parser details, and full Unicode class/casefold semantics)
- [P1][SL2] lower SMTP client transport and protocol handling into Rust intrinsics and add STARTTLS/auth/LMTP parity
- [P1][SL3] asyncio.tools re-exports graph introspection functions from asyncio; full parity pending deeper runtime integration
- [P1][SL3] asyncio.windows_utils provides PipeHandle/pipe/Popen wrappers; overlapped I/O semantics are simplified
- [P1][SL3] replace `_aix_support` top-level stub with full intrinsic-backed lowering
